name: Two Pointers
description: Questions on two-pointer technique for arrays and strings.

questions.question: Two pointers are often used to:
choices: Traverse from both ends or at different speeds
questions.explanation: Two pointers can move toward each other (e.g. two-sum on sorted array) or at different speeds (e.g. cycle detection).

questions.question: For a sorted array, two-sum can be solved with two pointers in:
questions.explanation: One pointer at start, one at end; move based on whether sum is too small or too large — O(n).

questions.question: Checking if a string is a palindrome is often done with:
questions.explanation: Two pointers at left and right compare characters and move inward.

questions.question: Fast and slow pointers are used for:
choices: Detecting a cycle in a linked list
questions.explanation: Floyd's cycle detection: slow moves 1 step, fast moves 2; if they meet, there's a cycle.

questions.question: Removing duplicates in-place from a sorted array can use:
questions.explanation: One pointer reads, one writes; write only when the value is new (e.g. different from previous).
